data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T201320-965c8275/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T201320-965c8275/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T201320-965c8275/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T201320-965c8275/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T201320-965c8275/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-494685af">
    <title>F (Foundation): Operation pattern + IDGenerator + sentinels + ctx access: part 3</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `*Store.Apply` method exists; type-asserts to `OperationApplier`; falls back to generic load-mutate-save. Test: `TestStore_Apply_DelegatesToOperationApplier_WhenAvailable`, `TestStore_Apply_GenericFallback_WhenNotAvailable`.
- `JSONLBackend` implements `OperationApplier` (empty switch + generic fallback — heartbeat-specific cases added in C bead). Compile-time assertion: `var _ OperationApplier = (*JSONLBackend)(nil)`. Test: `TestJSONLBackend_Apply_FallsThroughForUnknownOps`.
- Bead data model invariants (TD-027 §11.2) hold across `*Store`. Test: `TestBeadDataModel_InvariantsHold` asserts the 12 universal invariants on freshly-created and mutated beads.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `*Store.Apply` method exists; type-asserts to `OperationApplier`; falls back to generic load-mutate-save. Test: `TestStore_Apply_DelegatesToOperationApplier_WhenAvailable`, `TestStore_Apply_GenericFallback_WhenNotAvailable`.
2. `JSONLBackend` implements `OperationApplier` (empty switch + generic fallback — heartbeat-specific cases added in C bead). Compile-time assertion: `var _ OperationApplier = (*JSONLBackend)(nil)`. Test: `TestJSONLBackend_Apply_FallsThroughForUnknownOps`.
3. Bead data model invariants (TD-027 §11.2) hold across `*Store`. Test: `TestBeadDataModel_InvariantsHold` asserts the 12 universal invariants on freshly-created and mutated beads.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:iterate, area:bead, area:storage, kind:feature, kind:refactor, spec:FEAT-004, spec:TD-027, reliability, decomposed, triage:no-changes-unjustified</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-c6317784" base-rev="d66f9678d01fa3fd960aeb0633249ae6fbbf9876" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T201320-965c8275"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `be406bdf9` (`fix: add operation apply fast path [ddx-494685af]`).

What changed:
- Added `Store.Apply` with backend delegation to `OperationApplier`, plus generic load-mutate-save fallback in [store.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-494685af-20260512T201320-965c8275/cli/internal/bead/store.go#L546).
- Added `JSONLBackend.Apply` and the compile-time `OperationApplier` assertion in [backend_jsonl.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-494685af-20260512T201320-965c8275/cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go#L78).
- Added focused tests for delegation, fallback behavior, JSONL backend apply, and the bead data model invariant sweep in [apply_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-494685af-20260512T201320-965c8275/cli/internal/bead/apply_test.go#L44).

Verification:
- `go test ./internal/bead -count=1`
- Pre-commit hooks also ran the bead package test gate successfully during commit.
